Skip to the content.

Scratchpad

A pinned, menu-bar-resident dump receiver for macOS.

A pinned, menu-bar-resident desktop window for macOS that receives arbitrary data dumps from anywhere — HTTP, local socket, or a CLI pipe — and lets you pipe the most recent dump through any shell command. No language SDKs, no config. If your program can POST, write to stdout, or open a socket, it can talk to Scratchpad.

Install

Three install paths, listed in order of recommended preference. All produce the same app — pick whichever fits your habits.

Homebrew (coming soon)

brew install aaronmyatt/scratchpad/scratchpad

Homebrew strips the macOS quarantine attribute automatically, so the app launches with no Gatekeeper prompt. (Tap repo lands with TASK-32.)

Full Homebrew instructions →

curl | bash

curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aaronmyatt/scratchpad/main/install.sh | bash

curl doesn’t set the macOS quarantine attribute that browsers do, so the installed app launches without Gatekeeper friction. The script verifies a sha256 checksum against the published release before extracting anything.

Full curl install details →

Direct DMG download

Grab Scratchpad.dmg from the latest GitHub Release, double-click to mount, drag into /Applications. Hits Gatekeeper on first launch — easily worked around.

Direct download + Gatekeeper workaround →

Send a dump from anywhere

Two transports, same payload semantics. Bytes go in, bytes appear in the window. No headers, no schema.

# Bash — pipe via the bundled sp CLI (shortest)
echo "hello" | sp

# Bash — raw curl (no install required beyond curl)
echo "hello" | curl -X POST --data-binary @- http://127.0.0.1:8473/dump

# Python (stdlib only)
import urllib.request
urllib.request.urlopen(
    urllib.request.Request(
        "http://127.0.0.1:8473/dump",
        data=b'{"event":"login","user":"aaron"}',
        method="POST",
    )
)

// Node.js (stdlib only)
require("node:http").request({
  host: "127.0.0.1", port: 8473, path: "/dump", method: "POST",
}).end(JSON.stringify({ event: "login", user: "aaron" }));

Quickstart with more languages →

The shell input bar

The bottom of the window has a $ prompt. Type any shell command, press Enter — the most recent dump is piped to its stdin, and the output replaces the dump in the display.

Command What happens
jq . Pretty-prints JSON
wc -c Counts bytes
grep error Filters lines
xargs -n1 echo One token per line

Chain commands by running them in sequence — each pipes the currently displayed event, not just the original dump.
